core_mechanism: suppression of a long-wavelength transverse (modulational/undulation) instability of an extended one-dimensional defect by opening a spectral gap in its soft transverse fluctuation branch, so the interface acquires a finite bending stiffness that penalizes deformation
brief_reason: The abstract attributes stabilization of the otherwise transversely-unstable straight soliton to the lowest transverse fluctuation branch remaining gapped and to increased bending stiffness that penalizes transverse deformation, which is the generic mechanism of gapping a soft undulation mode to arrest a modulational instability.
